live_comparison.derived_winner_meaning: Component-level winners are recorded in the comparison matrix; there is no scalar overall winner field.
live_comparison.interpretation: Dexter is stronger on candidate generation, immediate post-signal action, slippage, realized upside capture, and stale-position control. Mew-X is stronger on fill latency, drawdown containment, time-to-peak, and holding closer to peak on exit.

replay_consistency.interpretation: The replay-surface repair preserves the same component-level comparison shape seen on the promoted live baseline rather than changing it.

caveats.replay_scope: Current zero-gap evidence is scoped to close-summary and position_closed realized-return fidelity rather than full execution-path equivalence.
caveats.mewx_scope: Mew-X replay parity remains a summary-fidelity claim because replay reconstruction uses exported session summaries from the same session state that emitted position_closed.
caveats.dexter_scope: Dexter replay parity now depends on the narrow added close-summary surface for promoted non-stagnant closes and should stay scoped to that repaired surface.
caveats.metric_scope_gap: The gap proof measures matched position_closed realized_return_pct only; Dexter realized_vs_peak_gap_pct shifts from 20.533046470504612 live to 25.804157291090654 replay while the close-return gap stays zero.
caveats.candidate_metric_asymmetry: Mew-X candidate_precision_proxy remains unavailable because creator_candidate is absent under the frozen-source export surface.
